steps.title: Use 6-hour periods
steps.content: A value that is $129\%$ greater is multiplied by $1+1.29=2.29$. Since the growth happens every $6$ hours, the exponent should count 6-hour periods: after $x$ hours, that is $x/6$ periods.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: At $x=18$ hours, there have been $18/6=3$ growth periods and the mass is $613.90$ grams. Work backward to the starting value: $$a(2.29)^3=613.90$$ $$a=\frac{613.90}{(2.29)^3}\approx51.12$$ So the model is $$A=51.12(2.29)^{x/6}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choices
steps.content: Choices A and D use base $1.29$, which means $129\%$ of the old mass, not $129\%$ greater than the old mass. Choice B uses the right base but the wrong starting value. Choice C has both model values, $51.12$ and $2.29$, so choice C is correct.
